entry.title: Multilingual Maintenance of G/L Account Texts in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Cloud Experts,

I have recently uploaded approximately 900 G/L accounts by copying from a reference G/L account using the new feature available in the app F0763B – Manage Operating Chart of Accounts, specifically through the Expert Worklist functionality.

As my logon language during this process was English, the descriptions were maintained only in EN. I initially assumed this would not pose a problem, as I intended to maintain the German (DE) and French (FR) texts later using the Renumbering Process.

However, I encountered an issue when attempting to do so via the app F3026 – Renumber G/L Accounts. The system throws the following error:

“Delivered ID and language combination not found in G/L account master data.”

This restriction is significantly impacting our efficiency. This is my second project within the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment, and I must highlight that the current G/L account maintenance process is considerably more complex and time-consuming compared to the On-Premise version.

I would appreciate your guidance on how to resolve this issue and your recommendations for best practices in such scenarios.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Oussama MEHENNI

entry.title: Employee role(BUP003) in BP is not showing in dropdown
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am unable to find BUP003 Employee Role in the dropdown when I try to create a BP.

After reading technical material, I have done:

1. SM30: T77S0 Table

2. Activation Switch for Functions of BP

3. Define Number Ranges for BP

4. Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges

5. Define Account Groups and Field Selection (Supplier)

6. Define Number Ranges for Supplier Master Records

7. Set BP Role Category for Direction BP to Vendor

8. Define Number Assignment for Direction BP to Vendor

9. Define Reconciliation Accounts for Employees in Role FI Supplier

10. Define Default Vendor Data for Employees in Role FI Supplier

11. SE38: /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE

12. SLG1: Got the message "Pernr 00000003 successfully synched into Business Partner."

But I still haven't found the BUP003 in dropdown when do BP again. Does anyone know which of the steps I missed or did wrong in the above steps?

Thanks 

Vincent

entry.title: Re: URGENT - MaxDB mess !!

entry.title: Re: Possibility of SAP workflow monitoring from Focused Run
entry.content:
Refer to SAP Focused Run Expert Portal, where you can find Job & Automation Monitoring.

Content section shows among other possible job automation types:

  • SAP Business Workflow (with ST-PI 740 SP22+) (preview)

This monitoring option allows the end user to connect transaction SWIA from Managed System and  Focused Run Job & Automation Monitoring

entry.title: Re: Integration of Joule with IAS for SAP SuccessFactors...
entry.content:
Hello vishalpatial,

it is correct, we emphasize to use Cloud Identity centrally and not one per application.

Often the separate Cloud Identity instances are related to the customer org-structure or responsibilities and the tenant-separation is used to cover this.

The Cloud Identity Services offer a more granular authorization concept since some time now, but it was introduced as a feature and is not active automatically for tenants which were provisioned before. Please check the documentation, search for "AMS" and "policy" or check the first 2 steps of this blog-post which describes to enable the policies for Cloud Identity itself.

The AMS policies allow you to restrict what the different users can see and do within Cloud Identity which helps you to get a similar separation as you have today with multiple tenants but with one user-base and one list of applications.

Cheers,
Gunnar

entry.title: Re: Change Management - Change record in SAP S/4 HANA 2023
entry.content:
@msamiullah 

Go to the path : SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Logistics - General -> Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) -> Change Records -> Settings for Change Records -> Define Change Record Types

Inside of this node select the Respective Change record type and click on the section Change Number Handling at Change record Header, here you input the entry at which status the change number (when the change record moves to particular status) wants to get generate.

Note: So far I have seen at New status (Which is stated status) change number won't get generate).

entry.title: Re: Can you pass sy-subrc as parameter in class constructor?
entry.content:
I've solved the problem by creating factory method, which correctly imports sy-subrc into helper variable, and then calls constructor.

CLASS-METHODS: throw IMPORTING VALUE(subrc) TYPE syst_subrc DEFAULT SY-SUBRC RAISING zcx.

...

METHOD throw.
   RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx EXPORTING subrc = subrc.
ENDMETHOD.

...

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  zcx=>throw( ).
ENDIF.

ble.

entry.title: Re: Hide ALV toolbar icons after refresh table display
entry.content: Thanks for the feedback. By the way, I also recommend not using "CALL METHOD methodname" which is obsolete, just don't mention "CALL METHOD" at all, i.e. use "methodname( )." The only form where "CALL METHOD" can still be used is where it's mandatory, i.e. the dynamic variant, for instance "CALL METHOD ('ZCL_CLASS')=>('METHODNAME')."

entry.title: Re: Multilingual Maintenance of G/L Account Texts in SAP...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Since you uploaded all 900 G/L accounts while logged in with English, only the English descriptions got saved. Now, when you're trying to add German and French texts through the Renumber G/L Accounts app, the system throws that error because those language entries simply don’t exist yet. That app can only edit or renumber things that are already there, it can’t create new language entries.

 

What you should do instead:

1. Log in in German (DE).

2. Open the Manage Operating Chart of Accounts app (F0763B).

3. Use the Expert Worklist again — upload the same accounts, but this time only include:

    • G/L account number
    • The short and long texts in German
      (No need to include all the technical data again)

4. Repeat the same steps for French (FR) just switch your logon language to French.

SAP will just add the missing descriptions for those languages it won’t touch the English ones.

 

Tips for the Future:

  • Uploading all languages? Do it in steps, switching your logon language each time.
  • Don’t use the Renumber app for text maintenance — it's really just for ID renumbering and assumes the language texts already exist.
  • Keep a spreadsheet with all G/L accounts and their texts in different languages — super helpful across projects.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Service Order automatic replication change pointer
entry.content:
Hello @raskolnico , hello @RMerker 

Your question seems to be quite a tough one for the community — which might explain why it’s remained unanswered for over a year. But I’ll do my best to help you both out.

Usually, even for Direct Output, change pointers must be active for the relevant Business Object (BO). Thus, I would start by checking transaction DRFIMG to check for the BO Service Orders if things are activated: Cross-System Settings > Change Pointers > Activate Change Pointers

Next thing you should check is the BAdI Implementation: DRF_OUTBOUNDAre the conditions and timings properly defined in your case?

Finally, the DRF framework doesn’t always hook into all document changes out-of-the-box. Therefore it can make sense to hook in with the ORDER_SAVE BAdI and to directly call "DRF_OUTBOUND_CALL". But that needs to be tested thoroughly. 

Let me know if that helped. If not, I'm happy to support you further.

Best, Rob

entry.title: Re: 2 simple questions about SAC table
entry.content:
Hi Jason,

It is not possible to sort on 2 measures in a table, sorting depends on 1 aggregation only. I am not sure what is your use case, but let's say you have revenue & margin as measures and customer as dimension, the sort cannot be on both revenue & margin.

However, we are working on providing sort on multiple measure/dimension aggregations, e.g. sort on revenue by country, and then by customer. You can vote on this idea if this corresponds to your use case https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/262839 It should be available with QRC3 release.

Regarding the filter on measure, it is not available for all models. If it is, select the table, scroll down in the builder panel to the filter section, select the measure and in the dialog, then chose the dimension aggregation and the operand. Again, we are working on delivering more capabilities on this for QRC3 release.

Thank you for your patience

Greg

entry.title: Re: Statutory Reporting France Italy
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

1. Check if the right country versions are active

For these reports to work, you need the localization scope items:

  • 2IG for France
  • 2IH for Italy

If those aren’t activated, these reports won’t show up. Your system admin or SAP contact can check this quickly in Central Business Configuration or through your project config setup.

2. Use the app "Run Statutory Reports"

Once the country versions are in place, go to the app F1518 – Run Statutory Reports.

There, you should find the official formats for France and Italy, including the ones you listed. Make sure you’ve:

  • Closed the relevant periods
  • Assigned company codes to the correct country
  • Posted some data (empty books won’t show anything)

3. Still not seeing the reports?

You might need to check the configuration under Statutory Reporting Settings. Sometimes the reports need to be assigned manually per country or company code. If you don’t have config access, ask whoever handles your CBC or system settings to help.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Auto update fiscal year in FB03 to current year
entry.content:
The default value comes from parameter GJR.

  • Check (SU3) your default parameter for this field
  • There is a flag in company code definition for 'Propose fiscal year' 
    (but as you always get '2022' it should already be set)
  • Check with your Abaper on how to update the default value (table USR05) if required

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Shiroh Kinoshita
entry.content:
My name is Shiroh Kinoshita, and I have been with SAP Japan for 25 years.   https://www.linkedin.com/in/shiroh-kinoshita-3869026/　It is a real honor to be nominated by my friend Lluis Salvador (@l_salva) in Barcelona to #BIF (Blog It Forward), a social game in SAP Community.

＊日本語版も用意してて、このページをスクロールダウンして頂ければ出てきます（Scroll down for Japanese language version）

During the SCN period, I posted over a hundred blogs, but I haven't posted anything on the new SAP Community platform, so I am more than thankful to Lluis for his invitation – that recalled my passion !  

I think generally participants from Europe and English speaking countries are majority in SAP Community.  But in order to directly convey my messages to people in the same time zone and motivate them to blog or network here, I decided to describe both in Japanese and English - not through a Machine translation.

*my avatar 10 years ago, which is more like myself than latest generative AI 🙂

My SAP Journey

In my SAP career, I have played roles such as a roadmap and SAP's ALM (Application Lifecycle Management) advisor for SAP customers, and before that I had promoted industry solutions for Discrete manufacturing, Mill products, and Utilities industries.

SAP users and stakeholders should collaborate to realize value, via making the SAP Community and SAP User Groups indispensable platforms. Through interactions with partners, customers, User Groups, and analysts, I have enriched my understanding about SAP, and it has made my SAP life much more fulfilling than just working for company KPIs.

SAP in Far East Asia - how do you imagine?

Let me introduce SAP in Far East Asia and Japan. In Japan, SAP has become an essential IT platform, especially among large and global companies, making it hard to find non-SAP users among the top 100 companies. Proudly, SAP ranked 20th in the 2024 Interbrand global rankings. https://interbrand.com/best-global-brands/

However, in Japan, SAP's brand recognition is still limited. For example, at my school reunions, few friends know about SAP.  And it took years for my family to remember the name of my company, SAP.  Unlike Apple, Google, Microsoft, and Amazon, SAP is not widely recognized outside the IT industry. In Japan and Far East Asia, many SAP champions and professionals may feel isolated compared to the well-established ecosystem in Europe.

*several thousands participants come together to SAP annual event, and 1500 - 2000 gathered 2024 Japan SAP User Group annual conference

Language in technology and academic topics in Far East Asia

I have spent years as the only Japanese member of one of SAP’s global team, then I dreamed in English at night and thought in English at work. However, for most of my life, I dream at night and think at work in Japanese.  So that, I understand the struggles of Japanese SAP customers with English world, which may be a common challenge in Far East Asia, including China and Korea. While machine translation and AI are improving, the difference in deep thinking in English remains significant. We will continue to face cultural and language differences in the global SAP world.

*Instead, in Far East Asia, many Technology and Academic books are published with our local languages, that is our big strength because we can deeply consider and discuss in our local languages.

Global Team Experience - dream at night & think at work in English

I once worked in a global team consisting of members from Europe, North America, Latin America, and Asia, developing after-sales customer-facing programs. This period helped me deeply understand the differences between countries. The fast-changing IT industry in America, the globally spread elite society of India, the rational thinking in Germany, the strong sense of camaraderie in the EU, and diligence nature in Far East Asia, like Japan, Korea and China.

Global team meetings are held daytime in Europe, early morning in America, and midnight in Far East Asia.  My life was completely opposite from my family and friends in Japan – work at evening / night and sleep morning / daytime.  It cannot work for many years.  Despite the challenges, it was a rewarding experience!

Social Media Networking - powerful tool if you are active

In Japan's SAP community, I, @shirohpossible, often meet people through Twitter (now X) before meeting them in person. I have connected with SAP-related individuals like @tkwhr, @baborin, @kmotoyoshi, @LeQ, and @_nyop, and we have had gatherings in Shibuya, Tokyo. These interactions have been enriching, allowing me to learn about SAP from various perspectives.

And, for example @Atsushi_kazumi, who is a board member of our customer and JSUG chairperson.  And with influential CIOs and IT analysts.  Only through SNS, we can connect with them directly, and sometimes get fast hand opinions from them and sometimes feedback to them directly.

Social media's speed and ease of connection are invaluable, especially for end-users who often feel isolated in Japan SAP ecosystem.

My Personal Interests  

Outside of work, I enjoy outdoor activities and often go mountaineering. I have climbed 70 of Japan's 100 top ranked mountains.  To maintain my stamina, I recently completed the 33km Diamond Trail Challenge.  I also enjoy visiting hot springs and exploring Japan's numerous islands.

* Japan Northern Alps in May, 2024

* Sun rising from Mt. Fuji - we call it "Diamond Fuji"

BIF Questions

  1. What would you study if you could go back to being 20? I would study English and foreign languages more diligently from a younger age to accommodate with Global IT world. I would study more about IT, including SAP.
  2. Best lesson learned from your parents? My elderly parents are my role models, always grateful and never complaining, living with a smile.
  3. What do you enjoy most about your job? Solving complex puzzles are fun for me.  I feel satisfaction when overcoming challenges. I once considered taking a long break for outdoor activities but found that work provides a unique sense of fulfillment.
  4. Interesting stories about products/services you are involved in? Developing after-sales SAP services with a global team, I found that regional differences were minimal, with most challenges being industry-specific.
  5. Recommended information sources? I agree with Lluis Salvador's recommendations. SAP Community and social media have great potential, especially for isolated professionals or end-users in East Asia.
  6. Future predictions for your field? Cloud will be the paradigm shift. The key is how to leverage it effectively and strategically. More specific and strategic discussions and case studies are needed, and a centralized approach to knowledge sharing is essential - CoE.

Thanks for reading my blog.  As a ♯BIF series,  would nominate Tetsuya Kawahara, AWS and an opinion leader in Japan SAP User Group.  I have not met him for 7 - 8 years, but his name came up with my mind when considering anyone to be nominated in this series.  He is a real best performer in our time zone.

Japanese Version  ここからは、日本語圏の皆さん向け、日本語バージョンです

私の名前は木下史朗　SAPジャパンで25年目になる　https://www.linkedin.com/in/shiroh-kinoshita-3869026/　バルセロナにいる友人のLluis Salvador @l_salva　からSAP Community BlogのソーシャルゲームBIF（Blog it forward）で指名をもらう事になった　SCN(SAP Community Network)時代はブログを百以上投稿していたが、新しいSAP Communityプラットフォームでは、全く投稿して無かったため、ついつい発信が億劫になっていたところ　お誘いをもらいLluisにはとても感謝している　

SAP Communityはどうしても言語・文化の理由から欧米やインドからの参加が多いと思う　だが、同じタイムゾーンのメンバーにもなるべく機械翻訳経由では無く直接の言葉で読んで欲しく、また参加・投稿を呼びかけたく、英語と母国語で併記する事にした

私は、SAPライフの中で、SAP顧客の伴走役、SAPのALM(Application Lifecycle管理)や最適ロードマップの提言役、組立製造業・素材産業・電力などの業界ソリューションの推進役などを経験してきた　SAPユーザー・関係者は、価値実現のためにはノウハウをお互い協力し合うべきだし、そのためSAPコミュニティやユーザー会は無くてはならないプラットフォームだ　知り合えたパートナー、顧客、ユーザー会、アナリストなどの皆さんを通して、日々のコミュニケーションに厚みを持たせる事ができ、職務のKPIのためだけに働くのと違って、何倍もSAPライフが豊かになったかと思う

 

極東アジアのSAP

さて、極東アジア・日本でのSAPについて紹介してみたい　日本でも大企業・Global企業中心に不可欠のITプラットフォームとなっており、Top100社の中から非SAPユーザーを見つける方が難しい程　そして誇らしい事に、SAPは2024年度Interbrandで世界20位　https://interbrand.com/best-global-brands/　

ただ、日本ではブランド力としてどうだろう？　中学・高校・大学の同窓会に出た時も、SAPを知る友人は一握り　また、私の家族でさえ、SAPの名前を覚えるまで何年か時間を要した気がする　Interbrand Top100社は、日本人でもほぼ知っていると思うが、どういう訳かSAPはIT業界関係者以外には知られてない非常に不思議な現象　私の母国では心のどこかでSAPが完全に受け入れられていないらしく、Apple、Google、Microsoft、Amazonの様に広く認知度やブランド力を得るには距離があると思われる　SAPと言えば、数々の角度から専門家や有識者がいて、エコシステムが充実している欧州と比べると、日本語圏・東アジアでは数の決して多くないSAP関係者多くが孤軍奮闘している感がある　

私は人生のうち、夜中に英語の夢を見て、仕事中も英語で思考する期間が何年かあった　SAPのGlobalチームでただ一人の日本人としてずっと英語で仕事をしてた数年間だ　それ以外の大半の人生では、夜は日本語で夢を見て、仕事中も日本語で考えてロジックを組み立てている　だから、日本のSAP顧客が英語で苦労する気持ちは自分も充分分かるのだ　日本だけでなく、中国・韓国という東アジア圏共通の課題だと思う（その代わり、東アジアの特性として自国語で考え生み出す力は本当に強い　本屋には日本語はじめ母国語の専門書が溢れている）　

日本含め東アジア全体として、この言語の壁に対する解決策・特効薬はなかなか無い　Cloud時代になると機械翻訳が即応してくれ、AI、スマホ、Cloud技術でどんどん便利になって行くが、やはり普段英語で深く思考する・しないの差は大きいのだ　私達日本人・極東人はこれからもGlobal標準のSAPの世界で、文化言葉の違いに悩まされ続けるだろう

グローバルチームでの活動期間　～夜英語の夢を見て、昼間英語で思索してた時期～　

日本人私一人、欧米・中南米・アジアのメンバーから成るグローバルチームで活動した期間があった　アフターセールスの活動プログラム・診断プログラムをグローバルメンバーの知恵を持ち寄って開発するチームだ　この時期は、最も深く国毎の違いを理解できた時期だと思う

アメリカの目まぐるしく変化するIT業界、世界中で活躍するインドの優秀なエリート層の社会、ドイツ人ならではの生産性・合理性を重視したビジネス、非常に相互理解が進み仲間意識が強いEU、日本はどこか思い入れ・夢・ドラマなど、人の思いを大切にするビジネスや企業文化（賛否両論あると思っているが少なくとも私は世界標準で見て、日本企業は思い入れや価値観に対し勤勉に動くと思っている　例：日本人の方には馴染みあると思える「プロジェクトX」の様な世界）、中国や韓国のやはり少しどこか日本に似た東アジアならではの勤勉性・日本とは違うアグレッシブさ、ロシアのやはり少し日本社会からは分かり辛い価値観

チームの会議は、欧州の昼間・アメリカの朝・日本の深夜の事が多く、昼夜逆転生活が続いた時期　さすがに体に堪えて、長くはこのチームでの仕事は無理だったが、本当に楽しい思い出になっている

SNSを通じた緩いつながり・スピード感

そして、日本のSAPコミュニティの中でも、実際仕事でお会いするより先にTwitter(現在X)で知り合うという事も多くなった　@tkwhr　@baborin　@kmotoyoshi  @LeQ　@_nyop　(敬称略)　といったSAP関連の皆さんと知り合う事ができ、何度か東京渋谷で集まりましょうという事になった　皆さん、Gartnerのアナリストの立場やSAPプロジェクトの一線・Japan User Groupのオピニオンリーダーで活躍する面々で、普段のプレッシャーを忘れて、そして様々な立ち位置からSAPを知れて非常に面白い語らいとなった

また、＠Atsushi_kazumi　さん、＠toukatsujin さんの様に、SAPユーザー企業の取締役やJSUG会長の立場、また著名な日経クロステックの編集者ながら、時折身近に接する事のできる事もSNSの良さだ

SNSの良さとして、やはりスピード感がたまらない　「メールを打って」、「恭しくネクター・スーツで相手を訪問して」ではなく、瞬時に考えの共有ができ、同じ関心事を持った人を話の輪に巻き込める

日本・極東アジアの人は一般的にこれが苦手だが、最近若い世代ではどんどん活用も増えている気がする　ただ、もっとエンドユーザー・お客さんのレベルでも活用して欲しい

残念ながら、日本ではエンドユーザーで、最もSAPシステムをどうしようかという孤立感の度合いが高い　アドバイザに恵まれない場合、信頼できるネットワークが無い場合時として、経営判断で多額の投資をしたSAPを活かさず辞める塩漬けにすると言った判断になる事が多い　

日本の企業ではSNS使用のガイドラインが非常に厳しいが、SNSに慣れた欧米人の使用スタイルを学べば、どうやったら会社や人に迷惑をかける事無く、素早くネットワークを構築し、スピード感ある情報収集をSNS通じてできるか体感できる

@l_salva　もSNSを通じて知り合った一人　SNSの縁が切欠で、この様な情報発信の機会得られて嬉しい

 

自分について

仕事を離れるとアウトドアが好きで、休日はよく山に出かけている　日本百名山と言う、北は利尻富士から、南は屋久島の宮之浦岳まで、名峰と言われ選定された百座がある　今時点、利尻富士、宮之浦岳、北アルプスの奥穂高岳、槍ヶ岳、立山、剱岳など合計70座まで達成した　何故か富士山だけは人が多すぎてまだ行けてない　また先月は山に登る体力を維持するため、33㎞　登り下り2500mのダイヤモンドトレールチャレンジ大会というのに出場完走してきた　長距離完走はなかなか気持ちの良いもので、来年以降もまた出場したくなった

アウトドアが好きだと、行く先々の温泉・美味しいものなど多様に楽しめる　最近は遠征が多く、SAP CommunityへのブログもSCNの時ほど出来て無いのかも知れないが

日本には、数多くというより無数の島があり、これら離島めぐりも好き　北から上げて行く　礼文島、利尻島、神津島、琵琶湖・沖島、沼島、対馬、五島・福江島、五島・奈留島、五島・久賀島、屋久島、宮古島、石垣島、波照間島、与那国島　この中の一部は日本人にも知られておらず難しいかも知れない　アイルランドのアラン諸島も忘れられない

 

BIFの質問へ

自分が20歳に戻ったら何を勉強したい？

英語を勉強しだしたのが前職の海外事業で必要に迫られ30歳前からで、やはりもっと若い時から英語・外国語をしっかり勉強しておくべきだったという反省点　そしてやはりSAPを中心としたITは好きで20歳に戻っても勉強していると思う

両親から学んだベストレッスン

既に老いた両親だが、周りの人に感謝して一切難しい事や不満は言わない　笑って過ごしている両親が一番の手本だと思う

仕事で一番楽しい事　理由

仕事は難しいパズルを解く様で好き　難しくても解けた時の達成感が嬉しく、自信になりさらに次の難題に取り組めている気がする　一時期仕事を離れてアウトドアを長期に楽しむ事も考えた　ただやはり仕事に変わる自分のパズルゲームは見当たらず、両立する選択肢を取っている

関わっている製品・サービスでの面白い話し

やはり、SAPの診断サービスを開発するチームでの経験　欧州、北米、南米、アジア、オセアニア　各地域のメンバーが一同に会して何度も新規開発するサービスについて議論したが、全く地域による差は感じ無かった　業界・商習慣による差はあっても、国毎・会社毎・地域毎の差は共通部分が大多数と言って良い程、差が無かった　

推奨できる情報ソース

これは私を指名したLluisの意見と全く同じ　https://community.sap.com/t5/welcome-corner-blog-posts/blog-it-forward-lluis-salvador/ba-p/14065604　

特に極東アジア、エンドユーザー企業など、孤立しやすい環境の人程、SAPコミュニティやSNSは潜在価値がある

自分の領域の未来を想像・予測すると？

やはりCloudがパラダイムシフトの原点になる　Cloudは「どう活用するか？」のノウハウが命　まだ確立できているユーザー企業は少なくこれから　AI含めて、どうサービスを活用するか、の議論はより具体的な考察やお互いの研鑽が必要　SAP関係者・ユーザーとも、地域・国・言語・会社のルール・業界特殊性などを理由に孤立している場合では無い　より多くの具体例やCoE的思考（ノウハウを分散させず、1か所にまとめる）が必要でしょう

#BIF 次の指名は

AWSの河原哲也さんにお願いしたいと思います　河原さんはJSUGテクニカル部会のオピニオンリーダーも務められ、7～8年と長らくお会いしてはいませんが、♯BIFの次の指名では真っ先に頭に浮かんだベストパフォーマーです

さて今後どのようにBIFが続くか楽しみです！

最後は我が同僚であるSAP Customer Evolutionの日本チームメンバーとの写真で締めたい

entry.title: Re: Disable Automatic Clearing in EBS
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

 

If some of your Wells Fargo bank statements are getting cleared automatically, and you want to handle them manually in FEBAN, you’ll need to tweak how the system processes those lines.

Here's what to check:

1. Posting Rules (OT83)

 

The system uses posting rules to decide what to do with each bank statement line. If it’s set to auto-clear, that’s your issue.

  • Go to OT83
  • Look for the external transaction code used in Wells Fargo’s bank statement
  • Check what posting type is assigned
    → If it's something like “Clear open items” or “Clear G/L account,” change it to something like “Post without clearing”

This will stop it from auto-clearing and leave the line for manual processing.

 

2. Mapping External Transaction Codes (OT84)

 

Make sure that the external transaction code from the bank file is correctly pointing to your updated posting rule.

 

3. Interpretation Algorithm

 

Sometimes SAP uses algorithms to auto-match things (like based on reference number, amount, etc.).

  • Still in OT83, check if there’s an Interpretation Algorithm assigned.
  • If there is, consider removing it, that’ll stop SAP from trying to be too smart about matching.

 

 

4. Reprocess in FEBAN

Once the config is changed, new bank statements won’t get auto-cleared anymore.
You’ll see them as “open” in FEBAN, and you can clear them manually like you want.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: SAP LeanIX integrating Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps
entry.content:
When Enterprise Architects and IT Security Speak the Same Language

This blog was co-authored by @Michelle10 (PM @ SAP LeanIX)

Dear community,

In most enterprises, IT security and enterprise architecture are two critical disciplines, each with their own priorities, tools, and perspectives. One is laser-focused on threat detection, compliance, and access control. The other is mapping capabilities, rationalizing applications, and shaping long-term IT strategy.

What happens when these personas complement each other – not just in theory, but in tooling?

That’s exactly where the integration between SAP LeanIX and Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps comes in.

But wait, what about my managed apps governed by my corporate identity provider? No worries, SAP LeanIX may be integrated with Microsoft Entra ID for discovery and keeping track which apps have SSO etc. as well.

 

Connecting Two Worlds

Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps is your frontline for SaaS security and governance to detect threat attacks that arise from your SaaS app interactions and reduce the exposure to such attacks. Furthermore, it helps security teams uncover shadow IT, assess risk, and monitor data flows across

SAP LeanIX, meanwhile, gives enterprise architects the visibility they need to manage complexity – mapping applications to business capabilities, tracking lifecycle data, and supporting IT transformation.

The magic happens when you combine them.

 

 

From Discovery to Decision-Making

With this integration, you can pull application usage and risk data from Defender for Cloud Apps directly into SAP LeanIX. That means:

Security teams discover new or risky SaaS apps…

…Architects contextualize those apps within business functions and processes.

 You’re no longer just identifying what’s out there - you’re aligning it with your architecture strategy. This turns ad-hoc discovery into intentional governance.

 

Business Outcomes

  • Eliminate shadow IT with traceable business context.
  • Support rationalization with usage-driven decisions.
  • Align security and strategy with shared data and mutual visibility.

 

Why It Matters

This integration fosters collaboration between two personas that often operate in parallel but rarely intersect. With Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps and SAP LeanIX, they do - bringing security and architecture into a shared conversation around risk, capability, and value.

The result? Better decisions, stronger governance, and a much clearer view of your SaaS landscape. All powered from the Defender and Entra ID APIs. Not too bad, huh?

This is how modern IT operates: cross-functional, data-driven, and strategically aligned.

 

Get Started

Anyone curious about uncovering shadow IT and making better SaaS app decisions? Just reach out to me and Michelle or leave a comment.

 

Cheers

Martin & Michelle

______________

References:

  • Defender for Cloud Apps REST API | Microsoft Learn
  • Microsoft Defender for Cloud apps API in Microsoft Graph | Microsoft Learn
  • Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (MDCA) Integration for SaaS Discovery | LeanIX User Docs
  • Microsoft Entra ID Integration for SaaS Discovery | LeanIX User Docs

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales Cloud v2 - Extension fields from Account fo...
entry.content:
Hi @Private_Member_695108 

You're right — SAP Sales Cloud v2 has introduced significant architectural changes compared to v1, especially when it comes to extensibility and how data flows between objects like Account and Quote.

Yes, you can use external hooks. They're the official way to enhance business logic or enrich objects like Quote with data from related objects (e.g., Account). And yes, you're right it involves a round-trip via CPI, which might impact the performance a bit.

I hope it helps.

Rob

entry.title: Re: How to see the full delivery request in external API...
entry.content:
Hello,

the standard behavior is for the "Request" and "Response" data text display to be limited to 1024 characters. The character limit can be temporarily increased to 2048, but will always default back to 1024 after the temporary period has expired.

Please refer to SAP KBA 3483526 - JSON request/response data in SAP Cloud ALM External API Management not complete

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Activity Reason in service contract (CRM 7.0)
entry.content:
HI @rpetrulis 

There are multiple options you have to implement a similar behaviour then in 5.0.
The first one is going to be a pure WebUI development. You likely need to enhance the UI component/view manually (e.g., BTADMINH, BTOrderDetails, or similar) and bind the Activity Reason to the correct model node if it's not standard. Make sure: The context node is bound to the correct BOL/GenIL model. The related enhancement set is correctly assigned in your customizing. 

The other way, is rather a work-around with a Z-field, and it needs to be analysed if your only goal is to store something like an Activity Reason. If so, consider creating a Z field directly on the contract header, especially if: You don’t need full Business Activity process logic. You're only using a small portion of the activity’s data model. 

Another question: Can you reconsider the use of business activity as subobject? There would be alternatives around that.

That are the ideas that come to mind for now. Feel free to direct message me to discuss that more in detail.

Best, Rob

entry.title: Re: Get started with the ABAP custom code migration process
entry.content:
Hi @OlgaDolinskaja 

Thank you for the quick response! We also created the OSS ticket 522271/2025 in order to further analyze those issues with the wrong scoping results.

Following object types we discovered so far where the scoping results are mostly wrong:

  • active ENHO Enhancements (BAdIs) aka. Z-Classes,
  • active Customer- and User-Exits (Includes),
  • ABAP Z-Classes part of Web-Dynpros,
  • ABAP Z-Classes implementing IF_WORKFLOW interface,
  • Workflow-Exits (ABAP Z-Classes),
  • active implementations of Business Transaction Events
  • ABAP Z-Reports that are generated from SQ01 Queries

Regarding the missing functionalities, we would like to request the following:

  1. Full Export:
    The scope cannot be fully exported. Currently, it is only possible to export an individual package to Excel. It would be useful to be able to perform a full export of all objects — for example, to discuss the results with other teams, capture comments, statuses, etc.

  2. Scope Transfer accross Projects:
    The scope cannot be transferred to another conversion project. If we start the S4 migration in another sandbox, we cannot transfer the scope that has already been defined to the other system. This would save us from having to redefine the scope again in the app.

  3. Validation Tracking:
    By “tracking,” we mean the ability to set a flag at the package level to indicate that it has been reviewed. At the moment, it is difficult to identify which objects have already been validated.

  4. Reference Transparency:
    Objects marked as “In scope by reference” should indicate which reference was found (this is known as usage analysis in SAP).

  5. Integrated Source Code Access:
    Clicking on an object should open the source code in Eclipse, similar to how it works with the Simplification Findings.

Best regards,
Ben

entry.title: Re: CREATED BY USER NAME IS NOT VISIBLE IN TEMPLATE FORM...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

This modification you want on the form has to be done exclusively by a technical consultant. If you want the “Created By User” field in that FIN_FO_CORR_INTDOC_ENTRY form to show the person’s full name instead of just their user ID, here’s what you need to do:

1. Download the form template

Go to the Maintain Form Templates app, search for FIN_FO_CORR_INTDOC_ENTRY, and download it.

 

2. Open it in Adobe LiveCycle Designer

Find the field that shows the user ID. Right now it’s probably linked directly to CreatedByUser.

 

3. Change the data source

That field is only showing the technical user ID because that’s all the standard data source has.

To fix that, you’ll need to bring in the user’s full name. SAP has a standard view called I_User where you can get the full name for a given user ID.

What you or your dev needs to do:

  • Create a custom CDS view (or key user view) that joins your current data source with I_User, matching on the user ID.
  • Add the full name to the form interface.

 

4. Bind the form field to the full name

Back in LiveCycle, change the binding of that field to your new FullName field.

 

5. Upload the form

Upload your updated form back into the system and test it. You might need to assign it in Output Parameter Determination.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Unable to find field in OData Service Explorer
entry.content:
About which system we're talkting about? In case it's SAP CRM, the "Category" field typically maps to: Subject profile & catalog (underlying table: CRMC_SVCTYPH or CRMC_SVCCAT). Or, in One Order objects like Service Request, it can be part of the classification (Category Model/Multilevel categorization). Typically, the standard BOL component used is BT, and you need to look under:

  • BTAdminH for general header fields,
  • BTCategory or BTSubject for categorization.

If BTCategory isn't included in your data model, you won’t see "Category".

Let me know if I can help or direct message me.

Best, Rob

entry.title: Re: Business Role Groups & Business User Groups
entry.content: @jim_boyden1Agree, this is just misleading and not a solution. As far as i can say those groups are some replacement for what was achieved by the former concept of authorisation groups via authorisation field "BRGRU". There you can add objects to groups and then reference those groups in authorisation objects to restrict access. If you check catalog SAP_A4C_BC_IAM_DISP_PC on BTP ABAP, you can see that restrictions for user and role using those groups via fields CLASS and ROLE_GROUP. Defined user and role groups are offered as values there.

entry.title: Re: Customer wants to allocate advance against the invoice
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Yes, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it is possible to automatically apply an advance payment (or down payment) to an invoice but it depends on how things are set up and how the process is followed.

For Customer Invoices (Accounts Receivable):

If you've already received a down payment from a customer, SAP can automatically apply it when you post the invoice. For that to happen:

  • The down payment needs to have been posted properly (usually through a down payment request and incoming payment).
  • It should be linked to the same customer, and ideally to a sales order or billing document if you're using the SD process.
  • When you post the invoice, SAP will propose that down payment for clearing if it’s still open and relevant.

You can also manually match them using the "Manage Customer Line Items" app if needed.

For Supplier Invoices (Accounts Payable):

It works similarly on the vendor side. If you’ve paid an advance to a supplier (say, through a purchase order), SAP can propose that payment for clearing when the invoice comes in. For this to work:

  • The down payment should be created through the "Manage Down Payment Requests" app.
  • It must be posted using "Post Outgoing Payments" and tied to the correct vendor or PO.
  • When you enter the invoice, SAP will check for open down payments for the same PO or vendor and suggest them for clearing.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: BAPI_CONTRACT_CHANGE is not updating the Net Price in Outline Agreement for Future dates
entry.content:
Hi Team,

we have a requirement to add the scales and condition amount in outline agreement. we are using BAPI_CONTRACT_CHANGE and it's working as expected but the net price in the outline agreement is not getting auto determined and this is happening only for future dates.

Example: today's date is - 6th May 2025 and I have created a new validity period for 7th May 2025 and added the scales and condition amount using BAPI_CONTRACT_CHANGE, so when my system date is changed to 7th May 2025 it has to change the net price in the outline agreement automatically but the Outline Agreement is having previous validity period's condition amount as net price which is not correct.

Note: we are triggering this BAPI from BTP system (UTC time zone), and SAP system is in PST time zone. mentioned this point to get my doubt cleared as I am thinking this might be a time zone issue.


Please suggest a solution. Thankyou!

entry.title: Through the Looking Glass: S/4 HANA Service Management: Transition to Item-Based Accounting
entry.content:
I am a product expert in the area of S/4 HANA Service Management. In this blog, I would like to share with you some insight into the new feature: Transition to Item-Based Accounting for Productive Customers. This feature is available as of 2023 FPS03 in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition..

This blog covers the following topics:

  • History of financial integrations in S/4 HANA Service Management
  • Transition use case
  • Motivation
  • Challenge
  • Overview of the solution
    • Core features
  • Example setup
  • Walkthrough of the process with application screenshots, step by step
  • Restrictions and Outlook
  • Appendix with completed process setup.

History:

Service Management in the S/4 HANA public cloud release only supports the financial integration with item-based accounting from the start. This is different in the private cloud edition. Here, the financial integration with the account assignment manager already exists in the business suite and has been supported since the previous releases.

Since S/4 HANA private cloud release 2023, Service Management supports the new financial integration to Item-based Accounting as well. However, it is only available for greenfield projects, which means that the integration is only supported for newly set up systems.

Motivation

Item-Based Accounting is the future financial solution and supports exciting new features as follows:

  • Advanced Execution Order Process
  • Event-Based Revenue Recognition and TECO status
  • Planned Cost and Revenue directly displayed in the service contract and service order
  • Financial Hierarchy on the Service Contract
  • Business Completion
  • Service Hierarchy (Collective Accounting)
  • Intercompany Order
  • Unplanned Items in the Service Confirmation

Additionally, Item-based Accounting doesn't need an internal order as an additional financial transaction.

So, the big question is how productive customers running Service Management already productive with the old financial integration (account assignment manager with internal order, WBS element or profitability analysis) are going to transition to the new financial integration of Item-Based Accounting.

Until S/4 Hana private cloud release 2023, feature package 3 it was not possible to just switch on Item-Based Accounting in a productive system and work on of the following reasons:

  • Existing documents could technically not be completed.
  • Service transaction with new financial integration would be created, but in the old financial integration all cost and revenue from follow-up service orders are posted to an internal order or WBS element of the assigned service contract; with the service order using item-based accounting, this would break the financial reporting of the service contract using internal order or WBS element.
  • The old existing transaction would use the new financial logic, which would lead to severe inconsistencies.

The Issue with Service Contract Assignment and Financial Reporting

When productive customers activate item-based accounting, their systems already contain service contracts using the old financial integration. Unlike service orders, service contracts normally have a very long life cycle and can be active for several years. The system also contains rather short-lived unfinished stand-alone or follow-up service orders, They might have their follow-up transactions in different phases of the process as well. Last but not least, there could be stand-alone service confirmation using the old financial integration with the account assignment manager.

In the old financial integration, the service orders used the internal order of the assigned service contracts to post cost and revenue so the service contract has a complete overview on profitability.

Due to the issues described above, it means that new service contracts and service orders must work together with old transactions:

  • An existing service contract using the financial integration with the account assignment manager must be assigned to a new service order using item-based accounting.
  • An item from a new service contract using new FIN might need to be assigned to an existing service order with old FIN.

Both use case are supported starting with S/4 HANA private cloud release 2023, feature package 3. The first with an automatic settlement rule, and the second only with manual settlement.

The Solution (Key Features)

Now, SAP provides enhancements that support a transition phase from the old financial integration (account assignment manager) to the new financial integration (item-based accounting).

  • Support to assign service contracts using the old financial integration to service orders items using the new financial integration and vice versa.
  • Ensure that existing service order transactions that already use a financial integration (account assignment manager or item-based accounting) stay with it and their follow-up transactions use the same financial integration as well.
  • Provide an automatic settlement rule to settle service orders using new financial integration to service contracts using old financial integration, which supports a complete financial reporting with profitability analysis (CO-PA) on the service contract.
  • Enable the use for specific features (e.g. unplanned items, fixed price service with an intercompany order, service hierarchy) that normally require item-based accounting to work with a productive, old financial service contract.

1. Assign the new service orders to old service contracts.

2. Settle to the existing service contract automatically.

3. To support cost-based CO-PA for the existing service contract in case of I/O or WBS element, the service contract must be settled to profitability segment. This settlement is already in place if cost-based CO-PA is used.

The Automatic Settlement Rule

To have a service order related cost and revenue financial reporting and also still support margin analysis in CO-PA for the service contracts using the old financial accounting, cost and revenue of the service orders items using item-based accounting assigned to these contracts must be settled to the service contract.

This can be done manually but for simpleness and automation, a settlement rule is provided by SAP.

It detects this special case and then settles the actual cost and revenue to the service contracts' accout assignment object (e.g. internal order, WBS element or profitability segment) automatically.

Automatic Settlement - Setup

For this to work the following must be set up in the section Settlement, newly added in the Service IMG.

  • Define a settlement profile.
  • Create a strategy sequence for automatic generation of the settlement rule.
    • Maintain the settlement profile in the strategy.
    • Assign the strategies in the correct priority, include CB1 for this special case.
  • Assign the strategy sequence to the service order.
  • Define a substitution rule for the service orders that assigns the settlement profile upon creation of the item-based accounting object.

Define the Settlement Profile

Strategy Sequence

Strategies in the Sequence

Assignment to the Service Transaction

Setting the Settlement Profile via a Substitution Rule

How it Looks in the System

Now let's take a look at the system, how it actually looks like.

We have two service contracts using the old financial integration with the account assignment manager.

The first contract uses an internal order 2000123.

We can take a closer look by navigating to the internal order.

The second service contract is 7000002511.

The WBS element is assigned on item level.

When we create a service order using Item-Based Accounting, both service contracts using the financial integration with the account assignment manager can be assigned to the two service product items.

Both contracts are visible in the Transaction History. You can see on item level that it's Item-based Accounting because the status of the account assignment object is displayed.

The target is to do automatic settlement of the item-based accounting service order to the two service contracts using internal order and WBS element with the old financial integration. For this we need some financial postings.

A follow-up service confirmation is created and completed with both service products taken over. The time sheets and service contracts are displayed in the transaction history. 

Using the transaction CATS7 it can be ensured that cost is posted for the time sheets.

Now we create the billing request document and the invoice of the service confirmation so we get as well some revenue.

We can see 1500 Euro revenue for both items.

If we take now a look into the app Manage Settlement Rules - Service Documents, we see a settlement profile is assigned with the respective attributes.

The settlement profile YS0001 is assigned. We can edit the settlement rule and check the details.

For the first item we can see that the receiver for the settlement is the internal order 2000123 which is from the first service contract.

You can see as well the settlement strategy sequence.

 

For the second item, the receiver is the WBS element WDF2_WBS_01 of the second contract.

This means that if the settlement run during period end close is executed, the complete cost and revenue is settled to the service contracts. hence, the CO-PA reporting for the service contract is intact.

The settlement run can be executed manually by the Run Settlement - Actuals app.

We settle in the first period of 2025 on 01/07/2025.

Showing the item 20 it can be seen that settlement was executed successfully.

Sender information:

Receiver information, the WBS element.

 

We can check the app Service Actuals that lists the financial data for service transactions in Item-based Accounting. Postings are 0 EURO.

Cost-Based CO-PA: Settlement from Internal Order or WBS Element to Profitability Segment

Now to get cost-based CO-PA data the internal order or WBS element must be settled again to the profitability segment. This is already existing settlement functionality which is then already setup if a customer uses cost-based CO-PA.

To give a complete view, I will run through this settlement process for an WBS element here as well and add an example setup in the appendix section of this blog.

With the app Run Settlement - Actuals we select the cost object type WBS Element and choose our WBS Element WDF2_WBS_01 to settle it.

After we executed the settlement run, the revenue and cost from the WBS element is settled to the profitability element.

The cost-based CO-PA data can be displayed in transaction KE24 Line Item Display - Actual Data.

Choose costing-based after calling the transaction.

The last settlement can be seen by the date and time. I executed this settlement on 01/08/2025 but for the posting date 07/01/2024. Navigate to the accounting and controlling documents to see the respective settled values.

This step completes the financial reporting for cost-based CO-PA. 

Restrictions and Outlook

There exist some restrictions that are planned to be worked on in a future release.

The following features are not supported for this transition feature to item-based accounting in S/4HANA private cloud edition 2023, feature package 3:

  • If you use a service order with item-based accounting, no revenue accounting and reporting (RAR) integration is supported as this belongs to the old financial integration.

This is especially true for the scenario where the service order originates from a solution quote and is part of a sold package. (The solution quote number is sent with the Order Revenue Accounting Item (RAI)). Hence, revenue recognition for bundle products, e.g. to sell a bundle for a server installation with a maintenance contract, is not supported. Customers who are using this RAR scenario are reconvened to continue using the old financial integration.

For a service order item using the financial integration with accounting object manager that is assigned to a service contract using item-based accounting, no automatic settlement rule is provided by SAP. The service order must be settled to the service contract manually.

  • Execution order items as standard items or a  service hierarchy are not allowed to be assigned to a service contract using financial integration with accounting object manager because the settlement does not consider the follow-up PM order cost.
  • If a service order is using the old financial integration, it’s not allowed to use the item categories that are only supported when item-based accounting is active in the service order. This is because whether a transaction uses old or new financial integration or not is decided on header level. The item categories that are only supported when item-based accounting is active are as follows: execution order items, price items, items with advance shipment and items in a service hierarchy with collective accounting..

Restrictions, Limitations and Outlook

There are some limitations and restrictions that are planned to be resolved in a future release. but you also have to be aware that some features working in the old financial integration are not supported currently with Item-Based Accounting.

One example is the Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) integration. especially if you use the solution quotation with follow-up service orders for sold packages. The solution quote number is sent with the Order Revenue Accounting Item (RAII in this case in the RAR integration and provides with this a special package revenue reporting. Currently Event-Based Revenue Recognition does not provide any comparable functionality. Hence, revenue recognition for bundle products, e.g. to sell a bundle for a server installation with a maintenance contract, is not supported. Customers who are using this RAR scenario are recommended to continue using the old financial integration.

For a service order item using the financial integration with accounting object manager that is assigned to a service contract using item-based accounting, no automatic settlement rule is provided by SAP. The service order must be settled to the service contract manually. It is currently analyzed if for this use case an automatic settlement rule is provided by SAP in a future release.

Execution order items as standard items or a service hierarchy with a execution order sub item are not allowed to be assigned to a service contract using financial integration with accounting object manager. This is because the settlement does not consider the follow-up PM order cost. Currently, only postings from service management transactions is supported to be settled. SAP works currently to solve this restriction with hierarchical settlement in a future release.

If a service order is using the old financial integration, it’s not allowed to use the item categories that are only supported when item-based accounting is active in the service order. This is because whether a transaction uses old or new financial integration or not is decided on header level. The item categories that are only supported when item-based accounting is active are as follows: execution order items, price items, items with advance shipment and items in a service hierarchy with collective accounting. A service transaction can use only one financial integration with its item.

 

 

 

Appendix:

Example Setup for Settlement of WBS Element to Profitability Element

This customizing is needed so cost-based CO-PA can be used as well for the service contracts.

Maintain Settlement Profile

Create Profile ZWBSPS.

Set the parameters as displayed below.

Maintain the Settlement Strategy

Create sequence ZWBS.

 

Assign the strategy to PSG (profitability segment).

 

Assign to Internal Order

The order type for the service contract is SAPS.

Example Setup for Internal Order to Profitability Element

Maintain the Settlement Profile

Create the settlement profile as defined below.

 

Assign Settlement to Internal Order

Add the Settlement Profile to the Internal Order.

 

Specify Default Settlement Profile for Project Definition

 

Define Strategy for Settlement Rule

Tips

SAP introduced a new user parameter CRMS4_SRV_CO. If you set it to X (true) in a system using the old financial integration with the account assignment manager, then in S/4 HANA Service Management for your user Item-Based Accounting is switched on. This is especially helpful if you would like to do some testing in the development or quality systems without switching on Item-based Accounting system-wide. Don't use it in the productive system!

Item Category, Settlement Profile and Event-Based Revenue Recognition Customizing for the Transition Use Case

Normally, it is not allowed that an item category which has an Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR) Key customized gets as well a settlement profile assigned. It just makes no sense to realize revenue for an item if I want to settle it anyway. But if Item-Based Accounting is switched on, it is clear that customers want to use the EBRR functionality on the existing items categories and not duplicate there item categories and have separate ones for Item-Based Accounting. So, in case we assign a service order item using Item-Based Accounting to an service contract with the old financial integration and it gets a settlement profile assigned, there is no error if an EBRR key is assigned additionally to this item category.

entry.title: Customer Check out report required from CCO machine
entry.content:
Dear Team,

A customer checkout report is required with the following data

  1. Customer Name

  2. Customer Phone Number

  3. CCO Invoice Number (from the CCO machine)

Note: All customers are one-time customers so all are not created in SAP Business one

Kindly provide the report at your earliest convenience.

entry.title: The rejection code needs to be updated at the notification level
entry.content:
The user has requested the following requirement:

  • The Work Order is in TECO status, and the respective Notification is in Complete (NOCO) status.

  • In this scenario, when the user updates the Rejection Status Code at the operation level, the same code should also be updated at the notification level.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: The rejection code needs to be updated at the notific...
entry.content:
This is not a standard feature
You need to consider a custom enhancement

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
Hi @thomasmakkink 

you can manually create a SMBC object in ADT by right clicking on the development package -> New -> Other ABAP Repository Object -> Business Configuration Maintenance Object.

entry.title: Where can managers see SF182 external learnings of employees in the ILX?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

where can managers see the externally approved training submitted with the SF182 form? In the legacy view, this was easily done in the team view. We have already submitted a request to SAP and received this response:

'We have received confirmation from our internal engineering team that the external learning request option has been removed from the manager view and added under the activity menu on the new learning home page. This is done to streamline the experience. Managers can still use the links menu to perform the same tasks.'

However, I still cannot find any option under 'Actions' for managers to view the external training of employees. External training only appears under 'Approvals' if the approval has not yet been given.

Does anyone have any idea where the manager can see the external training of their employees in ILX?

Regards
Guido

entry.title: Custom Consumption Complex CDS view to Enable the CDC
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am having a custom CDS Consumption view which was built on KONDN Joining with union of tables(KOTN901 TO KOTN922) and CDHDR and CDPOS table. This View I want to enable the CDC to replicate into SAP Datasphere. Please guide how to proceed on this.

 

Regards,

Venu
